description: Encrypting with ECB or raw CBC/CTR without authentication, leaving ciphertext malleable and patterns visible.
body: Failure mode: data is encrypted with ECB (identical plaintext blocks produce identical ciphertext, leaking structure) or with CBC/CTR and no MAC, so an attacker can tamper with ciphertext undetected and mount padding-oracle or bit-flipping attacks. Detection signals: visible patterns in encrypted images/data; cipher mode is ECB or a bare CBC/CTR with no tag verification; decryption never checks integrity. Do not use unauthenticated encryption on attacker-reachable data. Safer fallback: switch to an AEAD construction (AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305), bind context via associated data, verify the tag before using plaintext, and add a test that a flipped byte fails verification.
